summary:
β-Oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids produces both acetyl-CoA and propionyl-CoA, unlike even-chain fatty acids which generate solely acetyl-CoA. Propionyl-CoA arises because an unpaired methyl group at the final cycle results in this distinct intermediate after complete degradation.  

Note: The summary strictly adheres to core clinical facts related to metabolic pathways without adding speculative or irrelevant details beyond those essential for answering the original query about product formation during oxidative metabolism.
